canada.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four

income.definition: Median after-tax income of Canadian two-parent families with children
income.limitations: The income estimate is national and the family composition is broader than the exact MBM reference family ages.

costs.definition: 2023-base Market Basket Measure components and total threshold for the Toronto reference family of four, current dollars
costs.limitations: The MBM is a modest basic-needs reference basket for a specified family and region, not observed spending by the median Canadian household. Utilities and household energy are embedded in the shelter and other basket components and are not separately additive here.
missingEvidence: Utilities and household energy are not separately reported in the Canadian MBM input.

basicNeedsThreshold.definition: 2023-base Market Basket Measure total threshold for the Toronto reference family of four
basicNeedsThreshold.limitations: MBM thresholds vary by region and family size; Toronto is an explicit reference case and is not a national cost average.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income is below 75% of the official basic-needs threshold or measured essentials consume all income
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada Market Basket Measure
thresholds.limitations: A reference-family threshold does not describe every Canadian household.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income is below the official basic-needs threshold or essentials consume at least 70% of income
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada Market Basket Measure
thresholds.limitations: The 70% share is a transparent project threshold, not an official Canadian cutoff.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income is less than 150% of basic needs or essentials consume at least half of income
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada Market Basket Measure plus project sensitivity band
thresholds.limitations: The 150% and 50% bands are diagnostic thresholds, not official poverty definitions.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income clears the configured basic-needs and essential-cost pressure bands
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official Statistics Canada inputs
thresholds.limitations: A median reference case can be affordable while many households remain under pressure.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter is less than half the food basket
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada Market Basket Measure
thresholds.limitations: The calculation omits household-specific debt, taxes already paid, and non-MBM expenses.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter cannot cover the food basket
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada Market Basket Measure
thresholds.limitations: This is a reference-basket test, not observed food insecurity.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Food basket consumes at least 20% of after-tax income
thresholds.source: Project pressure band using Statistics Canada inputs
thresholds.limitations: Canada has no universal official food-share affordability cutoff.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter covers the reference food basket and its share is below the pressure band
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official Statistics Canada inputs
thresholds.limitations: Food insecurity can persist for households below the median or facing other essential costs.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes at least 50% of income
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada shelter-cost-to-income classifications
thresholds.limitations: The official classification uses before-tax household income; this diagnostic uses after-tax income.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes at least 30% of income
thresholds.source: Statistics Canada and CMHC housing affordability definition
thresholds.limitations: The official 30% definition uses before-tax income and actual shelter costs, unlike this after-tax MBM reference comparison.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes 25% to less than 30% of income
thresholds.source: Project early-pressure band below the official 30% threshold
thresholds.limitations: The 25% band is not an official Canadian affordability threshold.

thresholds.householdType: Two-parent family with children; Toronto MBM reference family of four
thresholds.definition: Shelter share remains below the configured pressure bands
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official Statistics Canada inputs
thresholds.limitations: Tenure, mortgage status, location, and actual dwelling costs can produce very different outcomes.

income.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
income.limitations: Median Census households are not the same statistical population as average BLS consumer units.

costs.definition: Average Consumer Expenditure Survey food, shelter, and utilities/fuels/public-services expenditures
costs.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Consumer Expenditure Surveys 2024
costs.limitations: These are averages across consumer units, not costs for the median Census household. Housing uses shelter plus separately reported utilities rather than the broader BLS housing total to avoid double counting.

basicNeedsThreshold.definition: Weighted-average official poverty threshold for a family of four
basicNeedsThreshold.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
basicNeedsThreshold.limitations: The family-of-four poverty threshold is not household-type matched to the all-household income and consumer-unit expenditure proxy.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income is below 75% of the poverty threshold or measured essentials consume all income
thresholds.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS Consumer Expenditure Surveys
thresholds.limitations: Income, threshold, and expense populations are not exactly matched.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income is below the poverty threshold or essentials consume at least 70% of income
thresholds.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS plus project share band
thresholds.limitations: The 70% share is a transparent project threshold, not an official U.S. cutoff.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income is less than 150% of poverty or essentials consume at least half of income
thresholds.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS plus project sensitivity band
thresholds.limitations: The threshold combines unlike household concepts and should be read as a national proxy.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income clears the configured basic-needs and essential-cost pressure bands
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official U.S. inputs
thresholds.limitations: A national central estimate does not represent low-income, renter, regional, or family-type outcomes.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter and utilities is less than half average food expenditure
thresholds.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS Consumer Expenditure Surveys
thresholds.limitations: The inputs describe different central tendencies.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter and utilities cannot cover average food expenditure
thresholds.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS Consumer Expenditure Surveys
thresholds.limitations: This is not a direct measure of food insecurity.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Food consumes at least 20% of post-tax income
thresholds.source: Project pressure band using official U.S. inputs
thresholds.limitations: The U.S. has no universal official food-share affordability cutoff.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Income remaining after shelter and utilities covers food and its share is below the pressure band
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official U.S. inputs
thresholds.limitations: Food insecurity remains heterogeneous and is not ruled out by a national proxy.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes at least 50% of income
thresholds.source: HUD severe housing-cost-burden convention
thresholds.limitations: HUD burden definitions use household-level income and housing payments; this proxy combines aggregate sources.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes at least 30% of income
thresholds.source: HUD housing-cost-burden convention
thresholds.limitations: The numerator and denominator are not household-level matched observations.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Shelter consumes 25% to less than 30% of income
thresholds.source: Project early-pressure band below the HUD 30% threshold
thresholds.limitations: The 25% band is not an official U.S. threshold.

thresholds.householdType: National all-household / all-consumer-unit proxy
thresholds.definition: Shelter share remains below the configured pressure bands
thresholds.source: Project evaluation of official U.S. inputs
thresholds.limitations: Renter, owner, mortgage, family, and regional outcomes differ substantially.

global.unsupportedReason: No valid globally comparable absolute household-income and essential-cost measure is available in the project.
